threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call the Overground the 'train' instead of 'the rail'. Locals only mention the bus when it's about the classic 55.
The 35 bus rolled in with a guy serenading the driver. By the time we hit Camberwell, they were having a full-on duet. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a local artist painting a mural on the side of a kebab shop while an elderly woman picked up fallen flowers from the sidewalk.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 3 AM and hear techno thumping from a nearby asphalt party. It’s like an unofficial rite of passage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they instinctively check BART delays before even stepping out. Tourists just complain about the heat.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy singing opera on the 31 Balboa, totally transformed the commute. The whole bus felt like a Broadway audition.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just walk into a bar and find tapas all day. Most locals eat lunch at 2, and dinner’s not until 9. It’s a different rhythm.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their baffled expressions when the trams don’t run on Sundays; locals just shrug and walk instead.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still instinctively check your phone for Tube updates while walking. The locals just glide to the bus stop with resigned confidence.
Caught a nodding bloke clutching a fish and chips box on the 59 last night. He starts chatting about the 'perfect crunch' while the bus hits every pothole.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted a crowd gathering at the corner of Herrfurthstraße, arguing about whether the new vegan café was worth the hype. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people sitting in a park, reading books by candlelight at dusk. It's like a mini festival of lumens, so cozy yet so unpretentious.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their skip of the 12 bus when it’s running late—no one who really lives here will waste time playing the waiting game.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy doing yoga on the bus this morning. He was so focused, you'd think he was on the mat at Mission Dolores Park.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are just for selfies, but locals actually gather in them to debate everything from politics to the latest vermut spot.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the Strret performers—local kids roll their eyes while the newcomers dig for change.
